summary: Iteration 3 frontend testing — Yamaha-style SelectedChannelPanel + ChannelPills + InlineEqGraph + CompCurve. All 14 critical flows PASSED end-to-end (panel render, pill selection, bank tabs PHY/VIRT, 5 colored band markers + readouts, band freq/gain/q inputs propagate to readout, MUTE/SOLO/EQ/COMP/LIM toggles, delay+unit+pan+HPF/LPF, fader→gain readout, EQ & Comp modal open/close, sel-reset, sel-close, regression: clicking channel strip header selects channel, regression: Routing Matrix tab, regression: localStorage persistence of delay 7.7 ms across reload while selection NOT persisted, regression: version switch confirm dialog appears).

issues: Vertical range slider uses deprecated CSS 'appearance: slider-vertical' — produces console warnings on Chromium. Consider migrating to writing-mode-only approach per Chromium message. Cosmetic, no functional impact.
Some Recharts container in PresetManager(?) emits 'width(-1) height(-1)' warning when comp-modal opens. Non-blocking but indicates a chart rendering in a zero-size container at some point.

action_items: (Optional, cosmetic) Replace deprecated `WebkitAppearance: 'slider-vertical'` on sel-fader with the standard `writing-mode: vertical-lr; direction: rtl` approach already partially in style to silence Chromium warnings.
(Optional) Investigate Recharts width/height(-1) console warning that fires when a modal opens — likely a chart inside a hidden/zero-size container.
critical_code_review_comments: SelectedChannelPanel.jsx uses JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(o)) for deep clone on every band/field update — fine for current data size but could be inefficient for larger states; consider structuredClone or targeted immer-style updates.
InlineEqGraph drag handlers use both setDraggingIdx state and draggingRef.current — minor duplication but functionally correct.
Selection state is intentionally NOT persisted (per spec) — verified via reload: panel disappears, but state.outputs (delay 7.7 ms) is restored from localStorage on re-selection. ✅
ChannelPills correctly renders only the active bank's pills, hiding the other bank's pills entirely (verified count 0 for hidden bank).
Channel strip header onClick wires through props.onSelect — works from both pill click and strip header click.
test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None — used default DSP state from dspStore
context_for_next_testing_agent: Modal close test-ids are `eq-close` and `comp-close` (NOT eq-modal-close / comp-modal-close). Version switch test-ids are `version-v16` and `version-v8` and clicking opens a confirmation dialog (Cancel / Switch) before the bank actually changes. Tab test-ids are `tab-channels`, `tab-matrix`. EQ band marker drag is pointer-event driven — the band freq/gain inputs (sel-band-N-freq/gain/q) are the reliable test entry point and they update eq-band-readout-N. The fader is a vertical range slider; programmatic value-setting from Playwright must use the native HTMLInputElement value setter + dispatch 'input'+'change' events for React to pick up the change.
rca of the issue: No application bugs found. Two transient false failures in the first test pass were entirely test-script issues: (1) used wrong modal close test-ids (eq-modal-close/comp-modal-close instead of eq-close/comp-close) causing comp-modal to stay open and intercept clicks for all subsequent tests; (2) fader value set without using React's native HTMLInputElement.value setter, so onChange did not fire. Re-ran with correct selectors and proper event dispatch — all 8 affected flows passed.
